text: Brucinium Chlorate Dihydrate (BCDH) - A new organic molecular salt crystal with non-centrosymmetric monoclinic structure, demonstrating piezoelectric and nonlinear optical properties for optoelectronic and NLO applications
intermolecular_interactions: Electrostatic interactions between brucinium cations and chloride anions
isostructural_analogues: Other brucinium-based organic salts (B5ST, BHF, BHM, DBSH)
experimental_method: Piezoelectric d33 meter (PM-300 piezometer), Kurtz and Perry powder method for SHG, Nd:YAG laser for LDT measurements, Fluorescence spectroscopy
property_ref_title: Structural, optical and piezoelectric investigation on new Brucinium Chlorate dihydrate NLO single crystal for optoelectronic, piezo-sensor, transducer and OLED applications

thermal_decomposition_stages.description: Removal of water molecules and chlorine ions
Breaking of C-O and C-C bonds in brucine

supramolecular_structure.1d_chains: Formed along (010) direction through C-H···O hydrogen bonds
supramolecular_structure.3d_network: Stabilized by weak O···Cl and O···O interactions

notes: Crystal structure obtained from the Cambridge Structural Database (CSD)
CSD Refcode: VOYVAJ, CCDC Deposition Number: 1823610
Crystal grown by slow evaporation technique from equimolar ratio of Brucine and Copper(II) chloride
Non-centrosymmetric space group C2 is crystallographically compatible with piezoelectricity and SHG
Although publication title refers to 'Chlorate', deposited CIF identifies compound as chloride salt
CIF serves as authoritative crystallographic source for the compound
Paper reports both crystal structure determination and experimental piezoelectric/optical characterization
Crystal possesses two-fold twinning about (001) with twin matrix correction applied during refinement
One chlorine anion and water molecules experience positional disorder at 0.55:0.45 ratio
Asymmetric unit comprises two brucinium cations, two chlorine anions and two water molecules
Empirical formula from CIF: C46 H61 Cl2 N4 O12 (unit cell contains 4 asymmetric units, Z=4)
Supramolecular assembly through N-H···O, O-H···O, N-H···Cl and C-H···O hydrogen bonds
Crystal exhibits excellent optical properties with 78% transmittance in visible region
SHG efficiency superior to standard KDP material, suitable for NLO applications
High LDT value indicates suitability for high-power laser applications
Fluorescence studies reveal emission in visible range with potential for OLED applications
